title: aira insularis
side_effects: Aira insularis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, which may manifest as hives, itching, and swelling of the face, lips, and throat. Prolonged consumption of Aira insularis can also lead to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high saponin content. In rare cases, improper use of Aira insularis may cause respiratory distress, characterized by shortness of breath, wheezing, and chest pain. Furthermore, excessive exposure to Aira insularis may trigger cardiovascular issues, including rapid heartbeat, palpitations, and in severe cases, even heart failure.
